---
title: "Project Planning"
author: "Jenna"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## *Data tidying:*

- Join receivers with players.csv to attach player position

## *Question Ideas:*

- What's the successful play on fourth down?

- Clustering yards after catch & route ran for complete passes, using location of target (x and y coordinates)

- Which team is most successful at converting on fourth down?

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

nfl_passes <- read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/36-SURE/2026/main/data/nfl_passes.csv")
```

```{r}
# Selecting player position from dataset
players <- players |>
  select(displayName, Position) |>
  arrange(displayName)

# Joins nfl_passes and players dataset to add position of target receivers
data_set <- left_join(nfl_passes, players, by = join_by("target_player_name" == "displayName"))

```

```{r}
# Generating dataset with completed passes during fourth down routes
route_outcomes <- data_set |>
  filter(!is.na(Position),
         Position %in% c("RB", "TE", "WR"),
         down == "4"
         ) |>
  group_by(route_ran, Position) |>
  summarise(avg_yards_after_catch = mean(yards_after_catch), avg_air_yards = mean(air_yards), avg_total_yards = mean(yards_gained), completed_pass_perc = mean(complete_pass))

# Display dataset
route_outcomes
```

```{r}
# Generates graph to illustrate total yards during fourth down completions based on different routes and positions
route_outcomes |>
  mutate(expected_yards = avg_total_yards * completed_pass_perc) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = route_ran, y = expected_yards)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~Position) +
  coord_flip() +

```

```{r}
nfl_passes |>
  filter(down == 4, !is.na(route_ran)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = target_x, target_y, color = complete_pass)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~route_ran) +
  theme()
  

```

```{r}
nfl_passes |>
  group_by(posteam, route_ran) |>
  summarise(avg_yards = mean(yards_gained)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = route_ran, y = avg_yards)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~posteam) +
  coord_flip()

nfl_passes |>
  filter(posteam %in% c("KC", "CHI")) |>
  group_by(posteam, route_ran) |>
  summarise(avg_yards = mean(yards_gained), completed_pass_perc = mean(complete_pass), expected_yards = avg_yards * completed_pass_perc) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = route_ran, y = expected_yards)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~posteam) +
  coord_flip()

```
